svg.text: The work of moving knowledge across boundaries so it becomes usable by people who didn't generate it. PHIs translate between research and practice,
between federal policy and local implementation, and between expert communities and the operating professionals who need to act on information.
Good translation gets knowledge used, which makes PHIs the
trusted go-to translator, which brings more demand,
partners, and reach, which funds more translation. The
Community feedback drives tailoring, which raises fidelity
and uptake, which deepens relationships, which brings
richer feedback next time. What keeps translation
Leveraging partner channels extends reach cheaply, which
raises visibility, which attracts more partners, which
broadens reach. Lets PHIs disperse far beyond their own
Pushing for broad scale and speed means less tailoring per
audience, so fidelity drops and the message distorts, so
uptake falls. Scale and fidelity must be balanced, not
Comms and dissemination are thinly funded; projects scale
only to the funding available, so demand outruns capacity.
Funding and leveraged partner resources are the relief
Crossing polarized boundaries depends on staying
nonpartisan; lean too far on a sensitive issue and you
lose access with some audiences. Careful, inclusive

text.tspan: The engine: good translation gets used, which funds more translation.
What keeps translation accurate: listening loops back into tailoring.
Lets PHIs disperse far beyond their own comms budget.
The central tension: scale and fidelity must be balanced, not maximized.
Relief valves: government funding + partner-leveraged resources.
Lever: careful, inclusive language. Threat: polarization.
